description_long: This is a roughly cylindrical stone with a weathered, irregular surface and a muted, earthy coloration. The stone features two distinct holes on one of its flatter faces, suggesting it may have been used as a primitive tool, weight, or architectural element. Its surface is pitted and uneven, with visible chips and natural inclusions, giving it a rugged, ancient appearance. The coloration ranges from pale beige to reddish-brown, with some lighter patches and embedded mineral fragments. The stone appears solid and dense, with no visible seams or artificial markings aside from the holes. Its overall form is utilitarian, likely shaped by both natural processes and human intervention.
description: A weathered, cylindrical stone with two holes on one face and a rough, pitted surface in earthy tones.
description_view_0: The face of the stone is visible, showing two prominent holes and a rough, uneven texture.
description_view_1: A side view reveals the stone's cylindrical shape and irregular, chipped surface.
description_view_2: Another angled view of the face with holes, highlighting the reddish coloration and surface pitting.
description_view_3: The opposite side view shows a more continuous, less interrupted surface with lighter coloration.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a boundary marker
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and pitting with some natural inclusions; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and pitted with visible chips and surface erosion, indicating prolonged exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with smooth man-made surfaces but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements.
